steps.title: Identify the grammatical structure
steps.content:
Side 1 is an independent clause (“Deposits of crushed volcanic rock...visitors” with a subject and verb) that ends at the blank.

Side 2 is a dependent clause starting with “When they take...” (dependent on the first clause for full meaning).

steps.title: Apply the rule for independent + dependent clauses
steps.content:
An independent clause followed by a dependent clause that begins with “When” requires no punctuation between them. Choice C adds a period and capitalizes “When,” turning the second clause into an independent clause, which violates the sentence boundary rules for dependent clauses.

The comma in B incorrectly separates an independent and dependent clause.

Periods in A, B, C misapply sentence boundaries.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
